title: Quarantine Release Audit
spl:
index=nac (sourcetype="nac:quarantine" OR sourcetype="cisco:ise:admin")
| search "quarantine" AND (released OR "cleared" OR "unquarantine")
| table _time, admin_user, endpoint_mac, action, ticket_id
| sort -_time
description: Audit trail of who released endpoints from quarantine and whether release matched policy (e.g., IT-only, ticket required).
value: Compliance teams audit every quarantine release — automatic (device remediated) or manual (admin override) — tracking which administrators bypass posture requirements and ensuring manual exceptions have documented business justification.
implementation: Map `ticket_id` from workflow; alert when `isnull(ticket_id)` and action is manual release. Join ServiceNow for approved changes.
visualization: Table (release audit), Timeline (releases), Bar chart (releases by admin).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Quarantines from BYOD enrollment, devices with stale certs, or compliance check failures during patch cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco ISE Quarantine and remediation logs in `index=nac` with `sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Key fields: `CallingStationID`, `SelectedAuthorizationProfiles` (tracks profile transitions), `PostureStatus`, `AdminName` (for manual releases), `AuthorizationPolicyMatchedRule`, `UserName`.
• Quarantine release can be automatic (device re-assessed as compliant) or manual (admin overrides via ISE Adaptive Network Control or Change of Authorization). Manual releases are high-risk because they bypass the posture check.
• ISE sends a Change of Authorization (CoA) to the switch when a device's status changes. The CoA triggers re-authentication, and the device receives a new authorization profile.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify quarantine release events:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-7d
| sort CallingStationID _time
| streamstats current=f last(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles) as prev_profile by CallingStationID
| where match(prev_profile, "(?i)quarantine") AND NOT match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine")
| stats count
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Quarantine release audit:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-7d
| sort CallingStationID _time
| streamstats current=f last(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles) as prev_profile last(PostureStatus) as prev_posture by CallingStationID
| where match(prev_profile, "(?i)quarantine") AND NOT match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine")
| eval release_type=case(
    isnotnull(AdminName), "Manual (Admin: ".AdminName.")",
    PostureStatus="Compliant", "Automatic (Posture Compliant)",
    1==1, "Unknown"
  )
| table _time, CallingStationID, UserName, prev_profile, SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, prev_posture, PostureStatus, release_type
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL: Identifies every device that exited quarantine and classifies how: automatically (device became compliant) or manually (admin override). Manual releases are auditable events — they mean someone decided to let a non-compliant device onto the network. This is legitimate for exceptions (CEO's device, critical production system) but must be documented. Unknown release type means the audit trail is incomplete.

Manual release frequency by admin:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-30d
| where isnotnull(AdminName) AND (match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)permit") OR NOT match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine"))
| stats count dc(CallingStationID) as devices by AdminName
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Quarantine a test device, then release it manually via ISE. Verify both the quarantine and release appear.
(b) Quarantine a test device, remediate it, and verify the automatic release appears.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Release type distribution. Row 2 — Manual releases by admin. Row 3 — Release audit log.
Alert: Immediate on any manual quarantine release.

Runbook:
1. **Manual release** — Verify business justification. Require ticket number.
2. **Same admin, many releases** — May indicate process shortcuts. Review with manager.
3. **Unknown release type** — Audit trail gap. Check ISE logging configuration.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **AdminName never populated** — Manual releases via ISE ANC (Adaptive Network Control) may use a different field. Check for `ANCAction`.
• **No automatic releases** — Posture re-assessment may not be triggering CoA. Check ISE Posture Policy > Reassessment settings.
grandmaExplanation: When a device is in the penalty box, it should only be let out when it fixes its security problems. Sometimes an administrator lets a device out early. We track every release, especially the manual ones, to make sure no one is cutting corners on security.
